channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth will remain blocked on a long-term basis under a federal judge's ruling in Seattle late Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump cutoff of humanitarian parole for immigrants from Ukraine, 6 other countries challenged
item.description: A group of American citizens and immigrants is suing the Trump administration for ending a legal tool presidents have used to allow people from countries with wars and political instability to come to the U.S. temporarily.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Father convicted in shooting of St. Louis youth football coach over son's playing time
item.description: Jurors have convicted a father in the shooting of a St. Louis youth football coach following an apparent dispute over his son's playing time.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alabama governor commutes death row inmate Rocky Myers' sentence to life in prison
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Alabama Gov. Kay Ivey commuted the death sentence of Robin “Rocky” Myers to life in prison Friday, saying there were enough questions about his guilt that she could not move forward with his execution.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Three men charged with plotting Las Vegas bus stop murder
item.description: Three men, Ta Jon Walker, Alexander Hinton, and Devante Reynolds, have been arrested and charged with murder for allegedly plotting the shooting of a man at a bus stop in Las Vegas in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, NewsNation

item.title: Texas GOP leaders defend appointee after tense exchange over diversity hiring
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — An appointee of Texas Gov. Greg Abbott wiped away tears and gave an emotional defense of her credentials after a tense exchange over diversity hiring policies, which was later followed by the state's top Republicans rushing to support her publicly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Iowa became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code on Friday when Gov. Kim Reynolds signed into law a bill that opponents say will expose transgender people and other Iowans to discrimination in all aspects of daily life.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York's governor orders college to remove Palestinian studies job listing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's governor ordered a state school to remove a job posting for a Palestinian studies teaching position this week, saying she wanted to ensure “antisemitic theories” would not be taught.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Patrolling America's 'third border' from the skies
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is taking to the skies to monitor Florida's coast as a crackdown on illegal immigration continues.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Leonard Peltier remains defiant in AP interview, maintaining innocence and vowing continued activism
item.description: BELCOURT, N.D. (AP) — More than 50 years after a shootout on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation landed him in federal prison, Leonard Peltier remains defiant.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Law school project finds slavery citations still being used today
item.description: EAST LANSING, Mich. (AP) — An 1842 U.S. Supreme Court ruling overturning the kidnapping conviction of a white man who seized a Black family and forced them into slavery south of the Mason-Dixon line is still being cited in American jurisprudence, 160 years after enslaved people throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump, Vance go off on Zelensky in contentious Oval Office spat
item.description: President Trump went off on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during an extraordinary Oval Office meeting in front of cameras on Friday in which he raised his voice and called the foreign leader “disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to pave part of the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Man charged after allegedly faking seizure as puppies were stolen
item.description: A 37-year-old man has been formally charged for his alleged role in the theft of two bulldogs from a Centennial pet store.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, NewsNation

item.title: Once-dominant video conferencing service shutting down
item.description: Skype, a groundbreaking service in the world of internet-based communications, will shut down after more than two decades of operations, parent company Microsoft announced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Commissioner Ford Jr. charged with bribery, tax evasion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Shelby County Commissioner Edmund Ford Jr. was taken into federal custody after turning himself in Friday morning. He is charged with bribery and kickbacks concerning federal funds and six counts of attempted tax evasion, according to a federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom, pave part of the Rose Garden
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to add a ballroom to the White House and to pave part the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Mexico kids forced to live in 'underground caves,' court docs say
item.description: A New Mexico home where eight children lived with their two parents was described by state police as looking like "a city trash landfill.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, NewsNation

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, KRON4

item.title: GOP Sen. Mike Lee suggests letting ‘pirates’ take on cartels on the border
item.description: He floated the idea that Congress and President Trump should allow private citizens, or "pirates," to take on international cartels to protect national security.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: USAID workers will be given 15 minutes to clear their workspaces as the agency gets dismantled
item.description: Thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development workers who have been fired or placed on leave as part of the Trump administration's dismantling of the agency are being given a brief window Thursday and Friday to clear out their workspaces.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Meet the only 2 passengers from the viral 'private' Southwest flight out of Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- It's not every day that a commercial airplane is transformed into a "private" plane -- but two Utah students got to see what it's like when that does happen.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WGN 9

item.title: Bell tolls as New York marks the anniversary of the 1993 World Trade Center bombing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Wednesday marked the anniversary of the 1993 bombing at the old World Trade Center, when Islamic extremists first attempted to bring down the twin towers with 1,200 pounds of explosives in a parked rental van.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MINCHILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Homicide fugitive from Washington state arrested in Utah by FBI task force
item.description: A 20-year-old homicide fugitive was arrested in Utah for the January death of a high school student in Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: 3 councilmen in South Carolina's third-largest city charged in bribery and kickback scheme
item.description: Three members of the City Council in South Carolina's third-largest city accepted kickbacks and bribes to give out grant money and rezone land or lied about what they knew, federal prosecutors said Wednesday, Charges against North Charleston councilmen Jerome Heyward, Sandino Moses and Mike A.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WGN 9

item.title: Judge criticizes Justice Department's broad reading of Trump's Capitol riot pardons
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday criticized the Justice Department's evolving position that a presidential pardon for a Kentucky man who stormed the Capitol also covers his conviction for illegally possessing guns at his home. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
